dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 2 for a convulsion at Apt 1, 1901 E Main St in [CITY].
Hi there, I'm Derek, an EMT with the rescue squad. Can you tell us what's going on with him?
He had a seizure-that's why we called 911.
Mr. Johnson, can you open your eyes for me?
Yeah... I think so. Just a moment.
Mr. Johnson, can you tell me where we are?
Can you move your hands for me, Mr. Johnson?
I don't see signs of obstruction or trauma on his face or neck. Airway appears clear.
Let me take his respiratory rate now.
His pulse is 59 and his blood pressure is 108 over 54.
He's slow to answer and sounds confused. GCS 14 might be noted.

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. (events leading up to the event)
dialogue.utterance: He was sitting up against the bed, then suddenly screamed and had a body seizure.
Pulse is 59.
Respirations are 18 and normal.
Blood pressure is 108/54.
SpO₂ is 96%.
Glucose is zero.
He has a history of Hodgkin's Lymphoma and a brain infarction a few years back.
He also has a history of primary hypertension noted in his medical records.
I'm going to apply four lead ECG monitoring now as per protocol.
We're going to attempt an IV start now, but I suspect poor peripheral access may be an issue.
We'll follow medical-seizure protocol 3-12.
Pulse has increased to 111-up from previous 59.
Blood pressure is now 144 over 96.
Glucose is now 163-up from the previous 0.
EKG shows sinus tachycardia currently.

dialogue.role: nose, mouth and throat exam; EMT
dialogue.utterance: Any discharge from sinuses, mouth or ears that might be important?
No cuts, bruises, or abnormal swellings. Seems normal.
He's feeling slightly weak and tired; no pain reported in other areas.
Mr. Johnson, can you rate your pain from 0-10?
My pain is 0, to be honest-I just feel tired but no real pain anywhere.
Got him secured to the stretcher-using all straps for safety. We'll proceed to [COMPANY_NAME] in semi-Fowlers.
Final reading during transport was 114 beats per minute.
Final blood pressure is 133 over 90.
All straps in place and motion secured. Ready for departure.
Confirmed clear to depart en route to the emergency department.
He's in semi-Fowlers and condition continues to improve during transport.
SpO₂ still 95.
Still 18, but sounded consistent during transport.
Thorax sounds clear and consistent with no adventitious noises.
He's in secured-position looks optimal as practiced.
Fully conscious now, and still no severe pain. Good sign.

dialogue.micro_intent: redo history of present illness (last oral intake)
dialogue.utterance: You drank one beer yesterday. Have you had any food or drinks since?
No deformities noted in the limbs. No visible back wounds or angulations.
Backing up to ED - already updated nurses about medications and readings.
He takes lisinopril, metoprolol, and aspirin daily.
He's allergic to Prevacid.
He will be transported in a semi-Fowlers position as per his comfort and airway management needs.
Pulse up to 114 now, consistent with earlier observations.
Final blood pressure is 133 over 90, showing stable trends.
All straps in place and motion secured. Ready for departure.
Confirmed clear to depart en route to the emergency department.
He's in semi-Fowlers and condition continues to improve during transport.
SpO₂ still 95.
Still 18, but sounded consistent during transport.

dialogue.role: nose, mouth and throat exam; EMT
dialogue.utterance: No obvious flaring nostrils or signs of infection in sinuses.
Neck seems stable and no signs of nuchal rigidity.
He has a history of alcoholism but has not been drinking heavily recently.
Skin is warm and dry with normal capillary refill and elasticity.
SpO₂ still 95 and looks optimally stable while in semi-Fowlers position.
EKG cleared with no further issues noted at this time.
Assisting to stretcher with minimal movement to prevent further stress.
We're making a cold transport to reduce oxygen demand.
He is able to follow our finger with both eyes and shows no signs of visual loss.

dialogue.role: nose, mouth and throat exam; EMT
dialogue.utterance: No signs of clear or bloody drainage; swallowing seems intact.
Full range of motion appears preserved and no signs of peripheral pulses compromise.
Normal bowel sounds and no palpable tenderness, rigidity, or guarding.
Multiple abdominal surgical scars but no new injuries identified during assessment.
Got him secured with all straps for safe transport to the emergency department.
He has not vomited during transport and remains asymptomatic.
Blood pressure remains at 133 over 90, with stable oxygen saturation.
He is still showing a stable improvement in mental status.
At this point, his overall condition is improving as we approach the ED.
No signs of dysrhythmia or other cardiac issues on the EKG monitor.
All set, just waiting for the ED to prepare.
Patient is now fully ready for handoff at the receiving facility.
Last verbal update to family: All protocols have been successfully followed.
Transport completed safely with no intolerable setbacks or delays.
All equipment and medical records accounted for and properly transferred.
Patient in stable condition, oriented and responding to stimuli during transfer.
All medications, IV attempts, and EKG readings have been fully documented.
Handoff complete and signed off by the receiving nurse and physician.
EMT notes and EKG traces handed over on completion for thorough documentation.
Transfer of care occurred safely at [COMPANY_NAME] Emergency Department.
Unit 2 is cleared from the incident and ready for the next call.
